cdp_scope3.reason: DHL runs its own fleet — backhaul on Einride reduces their own Scope 3 reporting gap.
Scania faces direct customer pressure on transport emissions — TRATON SBTi target 2030.
AstraZeneca reports Scope 3 to CDP and has a net-zero 2045 target for logistics.
SSAB is under intense pressure from Volvo/SSAB green-steel supply chain — Scope 3 Category 4 transport is a key reporting line.
Schneider Electric reports to CDP — 2025 target to reduce transport intensity by 25%.
Ena Energi subject to grid infrastructure reporting — green transport aligns with ESG disclosure.
Paper/pulp sector (SNI 17) faces Scope 3 Category 4 reporting from major retail buyers — IKEA supplier.
Biofuel sector faces EU ETS and Swedish climate reporting.
Kühne & Nagel is the 3PL — Einride reduces their reported Category 4 transport emissions for clients.
Nyby bruk (metals/steel, SNI 24) — SSAB and Outokumpu supply chain, SBTi pressure from downstream OEMs.
Volkswagen Group Sverige: Group-level SBTi 2030 target covers Scope 3 logistics.
Eberspächer (automotive heating systems) — Tier 1 supplier facing OEM Scope 3 cascade requirements.
Concrete production (SNI 23) — cement Scope 3 reporting by major construction clients (Skanska, NCC).
Martin & Servera: food distribution (SNI 46) — major restaurant chains require green transport documentation.
Fergas AB (industrial gases) — EU Industrial Emissions Directive drives green transport procurement.
Industrial estate (Jursla) with logistics/manufacturing tenants facing collective Scope 3 reporting requirements.
Tekniska Verken (municipal utility) — public sector green procurement rules apply.
